title: Why would monday.com change the edit view of workform?
excerpt: monday.com has changed the WorkForm layout when editing a WorkForm and as much as they thought it would make things efficient, it actually made it worse..

posts.author: creedhenry (Creed Henry)
posts.cookedHtml: monday.com has changed the WorkForm layout when editing a WorkForm and as much as they thought it would make things efficient, it actually made it worse..

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: What part is harder? Besides just getting used to something new, I actually prefer the new layout, it’s easier to move the questions around.

posts.author: creedhenry (Creed Henry)
posts.cookedHtml: My question really is who asked for the change? Sometimes I think we update things to the betterment of the company and not the users who use it daily. But this is just my opinion. I have to learn this new layout and go on about my day since the job doesn’t stop since I don’t like the layout.

posts.author: RopeDuck (Riolab)
posts.cookedHtml: I get how frustrating the change can be, especially for estate agents who rely on efficient workflows. While monday.com aims to improve usability, it might disrupt established processes. For more help on customizing WorkForms, check this monday.com support article learn more

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml: I prefer the new layout as well. It makes things a lot cleaner. I can now move questions in an easier way, I can actually see my edits a lot more clearly, and I can set conditional logic in a much smoother fashion. Also, the pre-filled data is a lot easier to visualize in my opinion.

posts.author: adamsmith12 (adam smith)
posts.cookedHtml: They likely changed the edit view to improve usability or streamline the workflow based on user feedback. Updates like this usually aim to make forms more intuitive and efficient for teams. monday.com have changed the trend.

posts.author: adamsmith12 (adam smith)
posts.cookedHtml: They probably updated the edit view to enhance usability and streamline workflows based on user feedback. monday.com is clearly keeping up with the demand for more intuitive tools. It’s great to see platforms evolve with user needs. For more related info on improving operational efficiency in property management, feel free to explore this resource.
